video_title: Michael Saylor: They Want You To Sell Bitcoin
transcript: 2026 is a great year because 2026 is the year where Bitcoin emerged as consensus global digital capital and no one really disputes that anymore. Digital credit is what allowed us to buy 175,000 Bitcoin this year in the middle of a bare market. Now we can see a path to plug Bitcoin into every bank, every money market, and every investor in the world through digital money. Bitcoin is digital capital. Digital credit is working. And the next exciting chapter in this entire story is the formation of digital money. That's the holy trinity in my opinion because if you can put together capital, credit, and money, then there's no reason why you can't. This episode has been so much fun to record, and I'm sure you will enjoy it. However, I noticed that more than 85% of you are not subscribed to this YouTube channel. And if there is one thing you could do to support us, please subscribe to this channel. It means a lot to us. Thank you and enjoy the episode. First of all, welcome to the show and uh taking the time here in Prague to spend some time with us. First question that I wanted to ask to you is how are you and what has been your experience over the past few weeks being in the markets and everything that has been happening surrounding you? >> It's all invigorating sometimes. It's a it's entertaining. Um the the markets are are pretty chaotic right now, but but one of the benefits of Bitcoin is it's always something interesting going on. And it's the most interesting asset in the world, which means that every minute of the day, everywhere in the world, somebody has something to say about it. If uh the president tweets and the tweet is a good tweet, Bitcoin goes up. And if it's a bad tweet, Bitcoin goes down. And sometimes when somebody says something just happened, I can tell whether it was a good something or a bad something just by looking at the Bitcoin price. Yeah. >> So, uh I think 2026 is a great year because 2026 is the year where Bitcoin emerged as consensus global digital capital and no one really disputes that anymore. The banks know they have to support it. The governments know they have to support it. Um I think this is the year that digital credit emerged as a real asset class. It's now more than 11 billion dollars. 12 months ago it was zero dollar. There was nothing. The entire asset was invented in the past 12 months and it's been extraordinarily successful, growing very rapidly and that is one of the killer apps on top of Bitcoin. Uh digital credit is what allowed us to buy 175,000 Bitcoin this year in the middle of a bare market. So digital credit is meaningfully meaningful driver of of the marketplace right now and supporter of the network. And then the third thing that's uh just really exciting is now we can see a path to plug Bitcoin into every bank, every money market and every investor in the world through digital money. Um, people used to dream a decade ago, they dreamed about a Bitcoin back stable coin, >> you know, and wouldn't it be great if I had a coin like Tether, but it paid me 8% or 6% or whatever. And no one could ever create one in one hop. You couldn't go from Bitcoin directly to the stable coin because Bitcoin was too volatile. And what we found with digital credit is if we go from Bitcoin which is like 40 V and 40 AR to a a credit instrument which is like five V and 10 or 12 yield. Then the third step of getting to zero V and five to 10% yield. That's the money step or the dig a digital currency that pays a yield is pegged to a fiat currency. That's an extraordinary thing. And you're starting to see entrepreneurs starting to work to create digital money that's pegged to fiat currencies backed by Bitcoin in the entire crypto ecosystem right now. That's more like a$1 billion segment. But you know, so Bitcoin's a trillion, digital credits 10 billion, 11 billion, and this money is one. But that's the holy trinity in my opinion because if you can put together capital, credit, and money, then there's no reason why you can't tap into trillions and trillions of dollars of opportunity. And that would be interesting to everybody in the world, every investor in the world in every jurisdiction. So for me, you know, there's the near-term chaos of, oh, there's a bare market and there's like, you know, a lot of people tweeting and a lot of other people about stuff, but the fundamentals of the industry are Bitcoin is digital capital, digital credit is working and the next exciting chapter in this entire story is the formation of digital money. If we if we talk about like the near-term, I think that the opportunity is really clear and even if we walk around at this conference, there's so many people coming here as the Bitcoin community and it's just expanding and expanding. But why is it that if we have consumer sentiment being so low and the S&Ps being at all-time highs that just Bitcoin is the asset that is lagging behind everything >> right now? The dynamic in the capital markets is you can think of Bitcoin as like risk capital squared. So normally things that are good that are good for risk or really good for Bitcoin like if interest rates drop suddenly and things that are bad for risk are really bad for Bitcoin. Um the difference between equity capital and digital capital is right now the equity capital markets are bifurcating into the into the winners the AI companies the SpaceX's the Google's the open AIs the anthropics the Microsoft companies that are digital monopolies with a distribution channel of a billion people that have that tech and they're like trillion dollar companies right So, if you look at the S&P, it's like, well, you've got like those trillion dollar companies. And then you've got the have nots, >> which are hotel chains and airlines and and retailers and traditional cash cows and even software companies, traditional software as a service companies. Investors are looking at those and saying, "Well, those are all not growth businesses or their targets." And so there's an AI bubble and we're living right now in the summer of the AI bubble or AI boom. $500 billion dollar of capital is being slurped into the AI complex right now. Google's raising 80 billion. SpaceX is got the SpaceX IPO is the biggest IPO >> in the history of the world. >> It's actually happening right now as we speak. >> Yeah. And so and so when you have SpaceX saying we want 80 billion and Enthropic wants 80 billion and Open AI wants 80 billion and Google wants 80 billion and all of a sudden Amazon's like yeah well we want 20 30 billion and then Meta's like well you know we think we might want 304 billion that's the public stuff in the equity market and then behind the scenes they're all pulling in hundreds of billions of dollars of private credit to fund these data centers. So what you have is uh a AIdriven black hole. It's orbiting through the capital solar system. >> Mhm. >> Massive gravitational pull. It's pulling credit money. It's pulling equity money. It turns out that it is it has pulled 10, 15, 20 billion dollars of capital out of the crypto economy. Mhm. >> You know, institutional investors have sold 5 to10 billion of ETFs and things like that. So, it's pulling some capital, but of course, Bitcoin is not funding all of this. Bitcoin is like 2% or 3% of the capital. That capital is being pulled into these hot deals. Every banker on Wall Street is out marketing the SpaceX deal, marketing the anthropic deal. And so then the capital investors think, well, I'll take my mature money that's been invested in the same business idea for three years, five years. Let's roll it into this hot new deal. >> Let's get in on it. I know I'm going to get a 10 or 20% pop because the bankers, you know, if they're going to do the deal, they're going to have to make the deal pop 10 or 20%. So, I'm going to get in on the deal and then I'll get out again. And so I think capital is is being pulled into all these hot deals. They're all competing for capital. When we get through, you know, IPO summer or AI summer, I think a lot of that hot money will come back into Bitcoin because Bitcoin is now undervalued. Mhm. >> It's if you want to look at Bitcoin, you can look at the premium or discount to the 200E moving average, >> which is kind of like the book value of Bitcoin or you look at the realized price. And what you can see is when the market's hot, it trades at 100% to 200% premium. And then when the market chills and the leverage comes out, it trades at that number or maybe at a slight discount. And so what you're seeing right now is this capital cycle. The more Bitcoin, you know, has capital drain out of it, the more compelling the price is, the better the value. At some point, those capital investors, they're not maximalist in anything. They're not religious ideologues. They can be excited about this deal this week. They'll be excited about another asset next week. And they're all looking at relative value. So, I think that equity markets have been performing well because they've been juiced by Nvidia and Google and some of the AI story and Bitcoin hasn't been getting that lift. But I think that after the after these uh these deals flow and after capital settles, I think that a lot of the capital will flow back into the Bitcoin and Bitcoin related securities. >> You know, probably the third quarter and I think by the fourth quarter things will be normalizing to our benefit. Is there ever a point where you start questioning the entire thesis of Bitcoin when it has been underperforming so heavily when you there's the old overlay of the M2 supply and Bitcoin and it's the gap is getting larger and larger and larger. Is there like a point where you think how are we going to proceed from here or might I be wrong on my te thesis? No, I feel pretty comfortable with Bitcoin right now and I make the point that we've been in the business since August of 2020 and if you pull up the Bitcoin chart since August of 2020, what you'll see is five major draw downs in five years. >> So, it's not uncommon for there to be a major draw down each year. This draw down is 50%. But, uh the draw down in was 75%. So, we've lived through more brutal drawdowns and it's to be expected. We were at an all-time high in October. >> Yeah. >> And so, you know, go back nine months and Bitcoin minimum of four year time horizon, but normally a 10-year time horizon. And I don't think uh I don't think you can let a price signal uh that's only a few months old drive things. If you're looking for a price signal, I would be focused upon like the fouryear simple moving average, the 200 week simple moving average because you got to have a four-year time horizon or more. But fundamentally, we're looking at the fun we're looking at the fundamentals. And the fundamentals in the last two years are the president supports Bitcoin, the vice president supports Bitcoin, the head of the Fed supports Bitcoin, the head of the treasury, the head of the SEC, the head of the CFTC. The accounting rules have been improved to Bitcoin's benefits. The tax laws have been improved to Bitcoin's benefit. The regulations have been improved to Bitcoin benefit. No, no bank was supporting Bitcoin two years ago. Now you've got 75% of the major banks starting to support Bitcoin. There were no ETFs two years ago. Now there's a hundred of them and they have a hundred billion dollars plus of capital. And so you there was no digital credit and there was no bank credit on Bitcoin. Now there is meanwhile we've gone through a having. We're two years from another having. So the fundamentals are much better. The support of the capital markets is much better. The support of the crypto markets and the and the bankers is much better. The competition is weaker. Bitcoin dominance was 41% in 2021. Yeah, that is 41% of all the crypto market cap was Bitcoin. In fact, it had been 70% and it fell to 20 41% and people thought there's going to be a flipping and maybe >> I know, >> you know, and so, you know, maybe Bitcoin's not the right network. And now, uh, Bitcoin dominance has has returned to about almost 70% 68% plus over those five years. It's a very unmistakable trend. And the trend is Bitcoin is the dominant digital monetary network. There's not going to be a competitor for that role. And is it going to be hacked? Is it going to be copied? Is it going to be banned? It's been copied 10,000 times. It's the winner. It hasn't been hacked. Everything else has been hacked. Bitcoin has been very stable and robust. Will it be banned? It's not being banned. And so if you look at the fundamentals, you would say this is something everybody needs. Nobody can stop. Most people don't understand. But that puts it in the category of Amazon in the year 2010 or Apple in the year 2012 2010. If you would ask a conventional investor, does you know home delivery Amazon network does it make sense for a retailer? They would have told you no. It's an awful business. It's going to fail. we don't need it. I don't get it. I'm not going to buy it. It was 10 years after it was short after fundamentally you could conclude that it was unstoppable. There was no company on earth that could could compete with Amazon in 2010 if you were honest. It took 10 years before conventional wisdom was of course we need Amazon. >> And with Apple, they created the iPhone and buy the iPhone 3 or the iPhone 5. It's jewelry. >> It's going to be the one of the great consumer products of the world and they're going to they're going to have, you know, this monopoly on a consumer network of a billion people and be able to control all the software and every every service that goes to a billion people. this isn't going to be a great company. Conventional wisdom disagreed. Everybody thought I'm crazy. And for eight years, people disagreed. And there was a point after about it was probably seven years later that people all of a sudden go, "Oh yeah, I guess Apple's a good business." And so with digital monopolies and digital networks, normally the network forms to the point where it's globally unstoppable.
